California Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Sueet, Suite 800

Sacramento, CA 95804-0807
RE: Town of Loomis - Request for Opinion Concerning LAFCO and LAFCO City
Selection Committee
Ladies/Gentlemen:
Pursuant to the provisions of the Political Reform Act of 1974 as amended ("PRA") the
Town of Loomis in Placer County hereby requests the commission's opinion on the
following issues:
1. May a member of the Placer County Local Agency Formation Commission
("LAFCO") who is appointed to LAFCO as a "city member" under Government
Code Section 56335 by the City Selection Committee vote on matters coming
before LAFCO pertaining to the annexation of territory to that LAFCO member's
city, when the person appointed has an ownership interest in a real estate brokerage
firm located in the area which is proposed to be annexed to that city, and where the
effect of the annexation may be to greatly increase the business done by his real
estate brokerage firm?
2. May the real estate broker refened to above vote on matters coming before LAFCO
if his brother owns land in the areas to be annexed to the city in which the broker
is a city councilman? Does it make a difference if the broker's real estate office is
located on one of these parcels owned by his brother?
3. May an altemate member of LAFCO selected by the City Selection Committee
pursuant to Government Code Section 56335 vote on annexation matters coming
before LAFCO when that altemate member owns a tract of land of 400 + acres
(either alone or together with other members of his immediate family) 2-3 miles
outside the city limits of the city of which he is a resident and council member,
which city proposes to annex several thousand acres of land to the city, where such
annexation may have the effect of greatly appreciating the value of such alternate
member's propeny?
Town of Loomis
(916) 653-1u40 . FA)( (916) 652-rA+7
6140 HORSESHOE BAR ROAD, SUITE K, LOO}IIS, CA 95t,5O


California Fair Political Practices Commission
July 10, 1991
Page Two
4.
5.
May a voting member (or altemate voting member) of a City Selection Committee
vot; to app6int o. ."*oue a City Seleition Committee appointment- to. LAFCO
where the City Selection Commit-tee member so voting does so in the hopes-.of
obtaining a La.fCO vote favorable to annexations to his city, where the City
Selectioi Member or altemtae member so voting owns property under the
circumstances described in paragraphs I through 3, above?
Under what circumstances does ownership of a residence in a c:ty bY - either
councilman serving as an altemate member of LAFCO or the City- Selection
Committee require-that the councilmen disqualify themselves from voting- on the
annexation when it comes before LAFCO- or appointment persons to LAFCO'
where the city in which they are councilmen prop-oses to arurex several thousand
acres to lt wtrictr annexation may affect the i:esidential property values of such
councilmen?
Do the provisions of Section 56636 of the Govemment Code override those of the
Fair Poiitical Practices Act which require that members who have a personal
financial interest of a substantial nature in a matter coming before a body of which
they are a member (in this case LAFCO) disqualify themselves from panicipating
in the matter when it comes before LAFCO?
**{.{.
The request of the Town of Loomis for the Commission's opinion on - th: above
enumeraied matters rises under the following facts and circumstances. LAFCOs are
established in each county pursuant to the Cortese-Knox Local Government
Reorganization Act of 1985,- as- amended (Covernment Code Section 56000 et seq.).
Among ottrer things LAFCOs are charged with the determination of local agency
boundlries includin! annexations of tenitoiies to those local agencies. Covemment Code
Sections 56001, 56017, and 56753.
In Placer County LAFCO consists of five members, two of whom are members of the
Board of Supervisors, one of whom is a member of the public at large appointed by the
other members of LAFCO, and the remaining two members of which are elected officials
of the legislative bodies of the cities ("city members") within Placer County. The city
members of LAFCO are appointed to LAFCO pursuant to Government Code Section
56335 of the Govemment Code. Basically, this iection provides rhar rhe City Selection
Committee established pursuant to Govemment Code Section 50270 et seq., which is
composed of the rnayors of all of the cities within the County of Placer, appoints city
members to LAFCO.
The. Qity selection committee in Placer County had not met since septembe r li , 1987 ,
until March or so of 1991. The exacr times at which they have met in l99l is a iubjeci
currently under invesrigarion. However,.in septembei of 19g7, the City seleciion
L'ommittee adopted a rotation system whereby the various cities within the cdunty


California Fair Political Practices Commission
July 10, 1991
Page Three
rotated terms on LAFCO and rotated "alternate" appointments to LAFCO on a calendar
basis established at the September 17 , 1987 meeting of the City Selection Committee. A
copy of the minutes of that meeting are attached to this letter.
On May 9 of l99l the City Selection Committee formally voted to remove the-Town of
Loomisi appointment to LAFCO and to appoint in her place the person- then.serving as
the Mayoi 6f the Town of Loomis. That May 9th meeting was attended by the mayors
or repr6sentatives of the mayors of Auburn, iincotn, Loomis, and Rocklin. Mr. Stan
Nadei, a member of the iincoln City Council, sat on the committee as Lincoln's
representative for the absent Lincoln Mayor Ron Barringer. Under the prelq! Clty
Selection Committee appointments Mr. Nader is the "alteriate" member of LAFCO who
votes in the absence of one of t}re two regular city members of LAFCO.
Mr. Ron Barringer owns a real estate brokerage firm located within one of the proposed
areas which th; City of Lincoln is attempting to annex in the near future. These
annexations must ali be approved Uv LlipCO. The total area to be annexed is
approximately 2,67'l acres and are shown on the enclosed map taken froq th9 I'incoln
G6neral Plal Public Facilities Element Draft Environmental Impact Report dated October
1990. The approximate location of Mr. Barringer's real estate office is located on the
map. The T6ivn believes Mr. Barringer also oiwns residential real property within the
City of Lincoln to which the 2,677 acies are proposed to be annexed, the street address
of such property being 540 "C" Street, Lincoln, Califomia.
Mr. Nader and members of his immediate family own the so-called "Nader Ranch"
approximately 2-3 miles north of the Lincoln City iimits along Highway-65. While this
ai6a is not currently proposed for annexation to tie City of Lincoln, the Town of Loonris
believes that given-thi massive annexation and development proposals which are to occur
within the so-ialled annexation area as shown on the map enclosed, it is highly probable
that the value of the Nader Ranch lands would be $eatly enhanced as a result of
Lincoln's successful annexation of the annexation area. As noted that Mr. Nader is
cunently the voting altemate member of LAFCO from the City of Lincoln. The Town
believes he also owns residential real property in Lincoln at 1554 First Street, Lincoln,
California.
Section 56336 of the Government Code provides that members of LAFCO are not
otherwise disqualified ftom voting on matters relating to their ciries, unless LAFCO has
alopted regulations prohibiting them from acring on proposals coming before LAFCO
that affect the LAFCO membei's city or district. - No sirch regulations have been adopted
by the Placer County LAFCO. In all evenrs, the Town of Loomis does not believe ihat
this section in any way overrides the provisions of the PRA as it applies to financial
interests of members of LAFCO in their individual capacities, and' ihat members of
LAFco are required to disqualify themselves fiom decisions 'in which they have a
qerqongl financial interest within the meaning of the pRA notwithstanding Government
Code Section 56335.


At the May 9th meeting of the City Selection Committee Loomis' representative Io
LAFCO, who assumed office under the rotation system previously discussed, after a vote
of the City Council to appoint her as the Town of Loomis' representative to LAFCO,
was ousted from that position by a vote of the four members of the committee then
present. Mr. Nader was one of the members voting to oust Loomis' representative and
ieplace her with the Mayor of Loomis. Mr. Nader was acting as Mr. Barringer's
representative at the time of the vote.
We are enclosing such additional information conceming the ownership of the Nader
Ranch and for Barringer Realty as we have available. Under the circumstances we have
not been able to clarify the eiact ownership interest of Mr. Nader and Mr. Barringer. in
either the real propeny or businesses in wh-ich they are involved as discussed above but
we believe tha[ both gentlemen own substantial interests in their business activities and
that the realty company is wholly owned by Mr. Barringer.
If you have any questions conceming the foregoing matters the Town of Loomis will be
pleased to attempt to answer them foi you. Thank you \ Vort attention to this matter.
Califomia Fair Political Practices Commission
July 10, 1991
Page Four
Very truly yours,
-' ...\
RobeqN. lo"foi J \ r'--r*
Town-Attorney
Town of Loomis
cc: Mayor & Council Town of Loomis
t


Mr. Ron Barringer
Is believed to be the licenseey'owner of Barringer Realty, Inc. Business located on
Assessor's Parcel 021-2?0-15-dO owned by Roi Barringer's brother and sister-in-law,
Oonald and Carol Barringer. These same parties o*n an -approximately 8 acre parcel of
land south of the realty-office within the proposed anneiation area. Parcels 021-27-
083-00 and 021-270-84-cic,. The parcel 'on'which Barringer Realty is located is
approximately 2 acres in size with a s-treet address of 271 Highway 65.
Mr. Stan Nader
Mr. Nader is believed to l>e a 22.2Vo owner of the "Nader Ranch" north of Lincoln with
a business address of 3500 Chamberlain Road. Assessor's parcel numbers for that
property include but may not be limited o the following: 019:290-10- (a0 acrel); -0J!1
2SdtS'(ttS acres); 02G15G30 (315 acres); 02G15048-(27.4 acres); 02G15049 (14.2)
acreq and 02G.150-54 (72.8 acres).
ENCLOSURE RE REAL ESTATE AND BUSINESS TIqLDINGS

sections.question:
1. May a member of the Placer County Local Agency Formation Commission ("LAFCO") who is appointed to LAFCO as a "city member" under Government Code Section 56335 by the City Selection Committee vote on matters coming before LAFCO pertaining to the annexation of territory to that LAFCO member's city, when the person appointed has an ownership interest in a real estate brokerage firm located in the area which is proposed to be annexed to that city, and where the effect of the annexation may be to greatly increase the business done by his real estate brokerage firm?

2. May the real estate broker referred to above vote on matters coming before LAFCO if his brother owns land in the areas to be annexed to the city in which the broker is a city councilman? Does it make a difference if the broker's real estate office is located on one of these parcels owned by his brother?

3. May an alternate member of LAFCO selected by the City Selection Committee pursuant to Government Code Section 56335 vote on annexation matters coming before LAFCO when that alternate member owns a tract of land of 400 + acres (either alone or together with other members of his immediate family) 2-3 miles outside the city limits of the city of which he is a resident and council member, which city proposes to annex several thousand acres of land to the city, where such annexation may have the effect of greatly appreciating the value of such alternate member's property?

4. May a voting member (or alternate voting member) of a City Selection Committee vote to appoint or remove a City Selection Committee appointment to LAFCO where the City Selection Committee member so voting does so in the hopes of obtaining a LAFCO vote favorable to annexations to his city, where the City Selection Member or alternate member so voting owns property under the circumstances described in paragraphs 1 through 3, above?

5. Under what circumstances does ownership of a residence in a city by either councilman serving as an alternate member of LAFCO or the City Selection Committee require that the councilmen disqualify themselves from voting on the annexation when it comes before LAFCO or appointment persons to LAFCO where the city in which they are councilmen proposes to annex several thousand acres to it which annexation may affect the residential property values of such councilmen?

6. Do the provisions of Section 56636 of the Government Code override those of the Fair Political Practices Act which require that members who have a personal financial interest of a substantial nature in a matter coming before a body of which they are a member (in this case LAFCO) disqualify themselves from participating in the matter when it comes before LAFCO?
sections.question_synthetic: The Town of Loomis seeks FPPC guidance on whether LAFCO and City Selection Committee members with financial interests in proposed annexation areas (including real estate brokerage firms, property ownership, and family land holdings) must disqualify themselves from voting on annexation matters, and whether Government Code Section 56636 overrides Financial Practices Act disqualification requirements.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from the Town of Loomis to the FPPC, not an FPPC response/advice letter. No CONCLUSION section exists. Document provides detailed factual background about specific individuals and proposed annexations. Minor OCR artifacts noted in text but do not significantly affect comprehension of the six legal questions posed.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: 1. May a member of the Placer County Local Agency Formation Commission ("LAFCO") who is appointed to LAFCO as a "city member" under Government Code Section 56335 by the City Selection Committee vote on matters coming before LAFCO pertaining to the annexation of territory to that LAFCO member's city, when the person appointed has an ownership interest in a real estate brokerage firm located in the area which is proposed to be annexed to that city, and where the effect of the annexation may be to greatly increase the business done by his real estate brokerage firm?

2. May the real estate broker referred to above vote on matters coming before LAFCO if his brother owns land in the areas to be annexed to the city in which the broker is a city councilman? Does it make a difference if the broker's real estate office is located on one of these parcels owned by his brother?

3. May an alternate member of LAFCO selected by the City Selection Committee pursuant to Government Code Section 56335 vote on annexation matters coming before LAFCO when that alternate member owns a tract of land of 400 + acres (either alone or together with other members of his immediate family) 2-3 miles outside the city limits of the city of which he is a resident and council member, which city proposes to annex several thousand acres of land to the city, where such annexation may have the effect of greatly appreciating the value of such alternate member's property?

4. May a voting member (or alternate voting member) of a City Selection Committee vote to appoint or remove a City Selection Committee appointment to LAFCO where the City Selection Committee member so voting does so in the hopes of obtaining a LAFCO vote favorable to annexations to his city, where the City Selection Member or alternate member so voting owns property under the circumstances described in paragraphs 1 through 3, above?

5. Under what circumstances does ownership of a residence in a city by either councilman serving as an alternate member of LAFCO or the City Selection Committee require that the councilmen disqualify themselves from voting on the annexation when it comes before LAFCO or appointment persons to LAFCO where the city in which they are councilmen proposes to annex several thousand acres to it which annexation may affect the residential property values of such councilmen?

6. Do the provisions of Section 56636 of the Government Code override those of the Fair Political Practices Act which require that members who have a personal financial interest of a substantial nature in a matter coming before a body of which they are a member (in this case LAFCO) disqualify themselves from participating in the matter when it comes before LAFCO?

embedding.summary: The Town of Loomis requests an FPPC opinion regarding disqualification requirements for LAFCO and City Selection Committee members with financial interests in proposed annexations to the City of Lincoln, specifically addressing conflicts of interest involving real estate broker Ron Barringer and council member Stan Nader, and whether Government Code Section 56636 overrides Political Reform Act requirements.
